prompt: isometric pixel-art game tiles for a top-down RPG map. Each tile is a DIFFERENT medium-sized object standing FREE on a small flat patch of clear blue water — the object itself rises straight up and IS the sprite. Do NOT put it on a raised block, cube, column, box, crate or pedestal, and do NOT draw the ground as a tall raised cube: just a small low patch at the base, everything else transparent. Go for maximum variety — different shapes, species, ages and conditions, no two tiles alike. The set: 1) a small wooden fishing platform 2) a rock topped with a gnarled shrub 3) a wooden dock post with a lantern 4) a mossy standing stone in the water 5) a tall clump of papyrus 6) a tall tuft of pampas grass 7) a wooden rowboat moored to a post 8) a cluster of tall bulrushes 9) a leaning driftwood snag 10) a heron nest atop a snag. cozy hand-crafted top-down RPG pixel art, rich natural texture, cohesive harmonious palette, soft directional shading, high quality game asset, transparent background
